SEARCH
Package grafiosch.gtnet.handler.impl

Class FirstHandshakeRequestHandler

java.lang.Object
grafiosch.gtnet.handler.AbstractGTNetMessageHandler
grafiosch.gtnet.handler.AbstractRequestHandler
grafiosch.gtnet.handler.impl.FirstHandshakeRequestHandler
All Implemented Interfaces:
GTNetMessageHandler
@Component public class FirstHandshakeRequestHandler extends AbstractRequestHandler
Handler for GT_NET_FIRST_HANDSHAKE_SEL_RR_S messages (incoming handshake requests). Processes the first handshake from a remote server that wants to establish a connection. The flow:
  1. Validate that payload and token are present
  2. Pre-process: reject if server not in list and allowServerCreation is false
  3. Store incoming message
  4. Create/update remote GTNet entry and update stored message link
  5. Evaluate GTNetMessageAnswer rules (if configured)
  6. Default to ACCEPT if no rules are defined
  7. On ACCEPT: generate our token, store in GTNetConfig, queue pending messages
When no GTNetMessageAnswer rules are configured for handshake requests, the default behavior is to accept the connection. Admins can configure rejection rules via the GTNetMessageAnswer configuration dialog to control which peers are allowed to connect.

  • Constructor Details

    • FirstHandshakeRequestHandler

      public FirstHandshakeRequestHandler()

  • Method Details

    • getSupportedMessageCode

      public GTNetMessageCode getSupportedMessageCode()
      Description copied from interface: GTNetMessageHandler
      Returns the primary message code this handler is responsible for. For handlers supporting multiple codes, this returns the first/primary code. Override GTNetMessageHandler.getSupportedMessageCodes() to register all supported codes.

    • validateRequest

      protected ValidationResult validateRequest(GTNetMessageContext context)
      Description copied from class: AbstractRequestHandler
      Validates the incoming request. Subclasses should check:
      • Required parameters are present
      • Parameter values are valid
      • Pre-conditions are met (e.g., remote domain is known)
      Specified by:
      validateRequest in class AbstractRequestHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      Returns:
      validation result indicating success or failure with error details

    • preProcess

      protected Optional<HandlerResult<GTNetMessage,MessageEnvelope>> preProcess(GTNetMessageContext context) throws Exception
      Description copied from class: AbstractRequestHandler
      Pre-process hook called after validation but before message storage. Can return an immediate response to short-circuit the template method flow (e.g., for early rejection when the remote server is not in our list). Default returns empty (normal flow continues).
      Overrides:
      preProcess in class AbstractRequestHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      Returns:
      Optional containing an early result to return, or empty to continue normal flow
      Throws:
      Exception

    • storeIncomingMessage

      protected GTNetMessage storeIncomingMessage(GTNetMessageContext context)
      Overrides default storage to first create/find the remote GTNet entry so that idGtNet is never null. For first handshake, context.getRemoteGTNet() is null because the sender is unknown, but the DB column id_gt_net has a NOT NULL constraint.
      Overrides:
      storeIncomingMessage in class AbstractGTNetMessageHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      Returns:
      the persisted GTNetMessage entity

    • processRequestSideEffects

      protected void processRequestSideEffects(GTNetMessageContext context, GTNetMessage storedRequest)
      Description copied from class: AbstractRequestHandler
      Processes request-specific side effects before response determination. Called after the message is stored but before auto-response is evaluated. Use for operations that must happen regardless of the response, such as storing a new remote GTNet entity during first handshake.
      Specified by:
      processRequestSideEffects in class AbstractRequestHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      storedRequest - the persisted request message

    • applyResponseSideEffects

      protected void applyResponseSideEffects(GTNetMessageContext context, GTNetMessageCode responseCode, GTNetMessage storedRequest)
      Description copied from class: AbstractRequestHandler
      Applies side effects after a response is determined. Called only when an automatic response is generated. Use for operations that depend on the response type, such as updating GTNet state flags after accepting a request. Default implementation does nothing. Override if response-dependent side effects are needed.
      Overrides:
      applyResponseSideEffects in class AbstractRequestHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      responseCode - the determined response code
      storedRequest - the original request message

    • storeResponseMessage

      protected GTNetMessage storeResponseMessage(GTNetMessageContext context, GTNetMessageCode responseCode, String message, Map<String,GTNetMessage.GTNetMessageParam> params, GTNetMessage replyToMessage)
      Overrides response message storage to use the processedRemoteGTNet from handler data, since context.getRemoteGTNet() is null for first handshake.
      Overrides:
      storeResponseMessage in class AbstractGTNetMessageHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      responseCode - the response message code
      message - optional text message
      params - response parameters
      replyToMessage - the original request message this responds to
      Returns:
      the persisted response GTNetMessage entity

    • buildResponse

      protected MessageEnvelope buildResponse(GTNetMessageContext context, GTNetMessageCode responseCode, String message, GTNetMessage originalRequest)
      Description copied from class: AbstractRequestHandler
      Builds the response envelope for the given response code. Default implementation creates a response message with the given code and message, stores it, and wraps it in an envelope. Override to customize response building (e.g., add payload).
      Overrides:
      buildResponse in class AbstractRequestHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      responseCode - the response message code
      message - optional text message
      originalRequest - the original request message (for reply linking)
      Returns:
      the response envelope ready for transmission

    • getDefaultResponseCode

      protected Optional<? extends GTNetMessageCode> getDefaultResponseCode(GTNetMessageContext context)
      Description copied from class: AbstractRequestHandler
      Returns the default response code when no GTNetMessageAnswer rule matches and no prior approval exists. Default returns empty (await manual review). Override for handlers that should auto-respond even without rules (e.g., handshake defaults to ACCEPT).
      Overrides:
      getDefaultResponseCode in class AbstractRequestHandler
      Parameters:
      context - the message context
      Returns:
      Optional containing the default response code, or empty to await manual review
